_notes.tour_attendance: Pulled live from DB via backfill_venue.py — games at non-home venues
_notes.nhl_market_score: 1-10. Based on NHL team presence, avg attendance %, season ticket demand, playoff history. Sources: ESPN, NHL.com attendance reports.
_notes.womens_sports_score: 1-10. Based on NWSL/WNBA presence and attendance, NCAA women's hockey proximity, women's sports culture indicators. Sources: NWSL, WNBA official attendance records.
_notes.arena_fit_score: 1-10. Based on availability of a 6,000-12,000 seat venue suitable for PWHL without being dwarfed. NHL arenas score lower (too big). Sources: Arena databases, team facility reports.
_notes.geo_balance_score: 1-10. How much adding this city improves league geographic spread. Current PWHL: BOS/NY/OTT/MTL/TOR (East-heavy), MIN (Midwest), SEA/VAN (West). More westward/south = higher score.

candidates.nhl_market_notes: Avalanche won 2022 Cup. Ball Arena routinely 95%+ capacity. Strong and growing hockey culture.
candidates.womens_sports_notes: No NWSL team currently (Colorado Spirit expansion incoming 2026). Colorado Mammoth (NLL) shows sports appetite. Denver strong women's college sports scene.
candidates.arena_fit_notes: Ball Arena (19,000) is too large for regular PWHL games but could host marquee events. Smaller alternatives limited — Magness Arena at DU (~7,200) is a viable home option.
candidates.geo_balance_notes: Only current US inland team is MIN. Denver fills a massive gap in Mountain West. Improves rivalry potential with SEA/MIN corridor.
candidates.narrative_hook: Denver showed up 13,562 deep on average — for games featuring teams they have no connection to. Imagine a home team.

candidates.nhl_market_notes: Original Six franchise. Legendary hockey culture. Red Wings averaged 97% capacity 2024-25. Hockey is religion here.
candidates.womens_sports_notes: No NWSL or WNBA team. Detroit has shown appetite for women's sports but lacks existing infrastructure.
candidates.arena_fit_notes: Little Caesars Arena (~19,500) is too large for nightly PWHL. However Detroit has Compuware Arena (~4,000) and other mid-size options. Could use smaller rink and upgrade for big games.
candidates.geo_balance_notes: Fills Midwest gap between BOS/NY and MIN. Natural rival to BOS, MIN, BUF (if added). Cross-border appeal with Windsor, Ontario.
candidates.narrative_hook: Original Six hockey market. 12,781 average at Takeover Tour games. Detroit doesn't just like hockey — it lives it.

candidates.nhl_market_notes: Flames are a passionate market, especially for Canadian hockey. Saddledome consistently near capacity. Strong corporate sponsorship culture.
candidates.womens_sports_notes: No top-tier women's professional sports league team but strong amateur women's hockey infrastructure. Alberta has produced many PWHL players.
candidates.arena_fit_notes: Saddledome (19,289) is too large for regular PWHL. WinSport/Markin MacPhail Centre (~3,000) and other mid-size venues exist. New arena (Calgary Event Centre) under construction.
candidates.geo_balance_notes: Canadian expansion is politically and culturally important for the PWHL brand. Fills Alberta gap between VAN and OTT/MTL. Natural rivalry with Edmonton and VAN.
candidates.narrative_hook: 16,150 fans showed up in Calgary for a single game. The city has been lobbying for a team. The data agrees with them.

candidates.nhl_market_notes: Capitals fanbase transformed under Ovechkin era. 2018 Cup win. Strong mid-Atlantic hockey identity. Capital One Arena 98% capacity 2024-25.
candidates.womens_sports_notes: Washington Spirit (NWSL) — one of the league's best-run clubs. Washington Mystics (WNBA) strong attendance. DC has the strongest women's professional sports culture in the US.
candidates.arena_fit_notes: Capital One Arena (20,356) is oversized. Smaller options include Kettler Capitals Iceplex (~1,200) — too small. EagleBank Arena in Fairfax (~10,000) is a realistic home venue.
candidates.geo_balance_notes: Mid-Atlantic fills gap between BOS/NY and southern US. However it reinforces east-coast concentration. Best justified by women's sports culture, not geography.
candidates.narrative_hook: 17,228 fans for a single game — third highest attendance of any Takeover Tour stop. Washington already has the best women's sports infrastructure in the US.

candidates.nhl_market_notes: Three Cups in six years built massive fanbase. 2024-25 rebuilding year but market depth remains. United Center one of NHL's largest and most profitable buildings.
candidates.womens_sports_notes: Chicago Red Stars (NWSL) — founding NWSL club, strong supporter culture. Chicago Sky (WNBA) — won 2021 championship. One of best women's pro sports cities in North America.
candidates.arena_fit_notes: Allstate Arena in Rosemont (~18,500 hockey) is large but PWHL used it. Wintrust Arena (~10,000) is near-ideal capacity. Chicago has excellent mid-size venue options.
candidates.geo_balance_notes: Fills US Midwest corridor between MIN and eastern teams. Chicago-MIN rivalry would be instant and intense. Central time zone anchor for TV scheduling.
candidates.narrative_hook: The biggest hockey market in America that doesn't have a PWHL team. Chicago Red Stars fans already showed up for women's sports. The infrastructure exists today.

candidates.nhl_market_notes: Hurricanes have built one of the NHL's loudest and most energized buildings over the past five years. Consistent playoff contender since 2019. Fanbase grew dramatically with sustained winning.
candidates.womens_sports_notes: NC Courage (NWSL) — four-time NWSL champions, based in Cary just outside Raleigh. One of the most successful women's soccer clubs in North America. Strong women's sports culture anchored by ACC universities.
candidates.arena_fit_notes: Lenovo Center (~18,680) is too large for regular PWHL games. No obvious mid-size NHL-quality alternative in the market currently. Would need a purpose-built or renovated facility.
candidates.geo_balance_notes: Southeast US is completely unserved by the PWHL. Raleigh fills a significant geographic gap and opens a new regional market. Strong ACC college hockey feeder culture in the Carolinas.
candidates.narrative_hook: 10,782 fans showed up in Raleigh for a neutral-site game with no home team. The NC Courage have won four NWSL titles. This market already knows how to support women's sports.

candidates.nhl_market_notes: 2019 Stanley Cup champions. One of the most passionate and loud hockey environments in the NHL. Blues fanbase has deep generational roots — this is a true hockey city.
candidates.womens_sports_notes: No NWSL or WNBA franchise. Women's pro sports infrastructure is limited. Strong NCAA presence via Missouri and SLU, but no top-tier professional women's league footprint yet.
candidates.arena_fit_notes: Enterprise Center (~19,150) is too large for regular PWHL play. Chaifetz Arena at SLU (~10,600) is a realistic right-sized alternative and already hosts hockey events.
candidates.geo_balance_notes: Central US anchor between MIN and the east coast cluster. Fills a major interior gap. Would create natural rivalries with MIN and CHI. Central time zone is valuable for national TV scheduling.
candidates.narrative_hook: 2019 Stanley Cup champions. 8,578 fans for a game that had nothing to do with them. St. Louis doesn't just support hockey — it bleeds it.

candidates.nhl_market_notes: Sabres have struggled for over a decade but fanbase remains loyal and passionate. True hockey-first city with generational roots in the sport. Lower score reflects team performance and market size.
candidates.womens_sports_notes: No NWSL or WNBA team. Limited women's professional sports infrastructure. Buffalo has sports appetite but no existing women's pro league presence to build on.
candidates.arena_fit_notes: KeyBank Center (~19,070) is too large. HarborCenter (~1,700) is too small. Mid-size gap is a challenge. New venue development would likely be required for a permanent home.
candidates.geo_balance_notes: Geographically close to Toronto, Boston, and New York — deepens the already east-heavy concentration. Cross-border proximity to southern Ontario is the strongest geographic argument.
candidates.narrative_hook: The most hockey-obsessed city in America without a pro team of its own. 8,512 fans showed up despite living an hour from Toronto. Buffalo doesn't need convincing.
